In 2019, he was elected as a member of the Northern Province Assembly and elected as president of his political party Le Rassemblement in April 2024.
Alcide Ponga was elected as president of the 18th government of New Caledonia since the Noumea Accord in 1998, in January 2025."},{"url":"https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alcide_Ponga","title":"Alcide Ponga - Wikipedia","content":"Alcide Ponga (born 1975) is a French politician who has served as the President of the Government of New Caledonia since January 2025.
He is the president of The Rally since 20 April 2024, and formerly served as mayor of Kouaoua as well as an executive in the nickel mining industry.
Ponga was born in Kouaoua, in 1975 into a Protestant family of Kanak origin that has been active for decades in New Caledonian local politics.
His mother served in local government, and his uncle, Maurice Ponga, was a Member of the European Parliament.
His grandparents were active in the fight for Kanak rights, while also supporting New Caledonia remaining part of France.
His influence within his party grew steadily, becoming president of The Rally in April 2024.
He lost reelection as mayor in the first round of the 2026 local elections.
Nouvelle-Calédonie la 1ère (in French)."},{"url":"https://www.theguardian.com/world/2025/jan/09/new-caledonia-congress-elects-alcide-ponga-president","title":"New Caledonia Congress elects pro-France president after political crisis | New Caledonia | The Guardian","content":"Weeks after its first ever pro-independence government collapsed, New Caledonia has named a French loyalist as its new president as the territory seeks to rebuild from deadly riots and destruction that battered the economy.
Alcide Ponga, from the Le Rassemblement party, was elected president by the newly installed executive in Congress on Wednesday.
Ponga’s party is affiliated with French right-wing party LR, Les Républicains.
The appointment brings some political stability to New Caledonia, a territory of France which lies in the Pacific Ocean, after its government fell on 24 December 2024.
The previous pro-independence government led by Louis Mapou had survived several major trials – including Covid and a referendum on full sovereignty – but the deadly riots in 2024 damaged the Congress and was a factor in its collapse."},{"url":"https://www.rfi.fr/en/france/20250110-who-is-alcide-ponga-new-caledonia-s-new-pro-french-president","title":"Who is Alcide Ponga, New Caledonia's new pro-French president? - RFI","content":"Ponga was named on Wednesday, 8 January, following the collapse of New Caledonia's first pro-independence government in December.
Like his predecessor Louis Mapou, Ponga is an indigenous Kanak.
Hailing from the Le Rassemblement party – which is affiliated to the French conservative party, Les Republicains – his political family is in favour of keeping New Caledonia within France.
“What I feel above all is the weight of responsibility,” Ponga said on Wednesday, after receiving the support of six of the newly installed government's 11 members.
Ponga is charged with trying to restore some political stability to the Pacific territory following the deadly violence that broke out in May."}]}

Alcide Ponga, 49, is the first indigenous Kanak to lead","score":0.97417,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=rImdTb5oI1A","title":"Alcide Ponga : "2026 sera plus dure que 2025" – Crise, Dette et Réformes en Nouvelle-Calédonie","content":"Alcide Ponga : "2026 sera plus dure que 2025" – Crise, Dette et Réformes en Nouvelle-Calédonie

4 Jan 2026
Invité du journal télévisé ce dimanche 4 janvier 2026, le président du gouvernement calédonien, Alcide Ponga, dresse un bilan sans concession de la situation économique du territoire. Entre les conséquences des émeutes, la crise du nickel et un budget sous tension, découvrez pourquoi l'année à venir s'annonce cruciale pour l'avenir de la Nouvelle-Calédonie.

• Une année 2026 difficile : Alcide Ponga prévient que les difficultés économiques persisteront, voire s'aggraveront par rapport à 2025, en raison de la baisse des recettes fiscales et des impacts durables de la crise.
• Réformes impopulaires mais nécessaires : Le gouvernement de transition assume de prendre des décisions difficiles (fin de niches fiscales, protections de marché) pour assainir la trajectoire du pays avant les prochaines élections.
• Dette et aide de l'État : Alors que les discussions se poursuivent avec l'État pour débloquer les aides, le président confirme l'incapacité actuelle de la Nouvelle-Calédonie à rembourser ses prêts, un constat partagé par l'ensemble de la classe politique.
